title: Phase 20: PSM 0x0010 IDA-confirmed RAD class identity + sub-kind discriminator
objective: Reverse-engineer the real RAD class behind PSM 0x0010 using IDA, identify the sub-kind discriminator offset + data type, enumerate sub-kind values, and produce an authoritative analysis doc mirroring Phase 16 jstyleoverride-v3-fields.md. Pure reverse engineering + documentation — no src/ code changes, no test changes.
evidence: Created after Phase 19 (leading_word audit) shipped and was committed (6beb6f1). Phase 19 probe confirmed 0x0010 is polymorphic with partial sub-kind discriminator at +0..+1 (28% coverage); heterogeneous size buckets (31/70/13/16/43) require IDA evidence to identify the real discriminator strategy. User authorized continuation via MCP relay channel.

summary: 12 reachable IDA instances confirmed via list_instances. Primary entry: radsrvitem.dll (port 13346, 5374 functions, 4867 unnamed). Secondary candidates: J2DSrv.dll (13347), style.dll (13348, Phase 16 navigated), sppid.dll (13341), smartplantpid.exe (13342), sppidautomation.dll (13340), sppiddwgprocess.dll (13343), sppidautomation.exe (13344), llama.dll (13345), ipidobjectmanagerinf.dll (13339), sppidautomationwrap.dll (13338). core.dll (13337) is AVEVA E3D, likely unrelated to SmartPlant P&ID. No new instance load planned.

radsrvitem.dll: 32-bit, base 0x56440000, image 0x3ec000. 5374 functions, 346 named, 4867 unnamed. 1739 strings. Exports: GetServerItemTransceiver (0x56448040), GetServerItemVersion (0x564480d0). DllEntryPoint at 0x564439e7. Segments: .text 0x56441000-0x5665e000 (0x21d000), .rdata 0x5665e578-0x5667b000, .data 0x5667b000-0x5681e000. Next: search_text for literal 0x10/0x0010/PSM dispatch pattern in .text.

Goal package drafted (brief/plan/verification/blockers/goal-prompt + 4 progress entries). Awaiting user authorization for /goal execution. Phase 20 estimated workload: 2-5 sessions (Phase 16 single type code reverse took multiple sessions; 0x0010 polymorphic family is larger scope). Recommended Slice A first action: search_text in radsrvitem.dll for PSM dispatch table referencing 0x10. Not commencing Slice A in this session per Stop-And-Ask scope (multi-hour IDA dive needs explicit /goal authorization).

Initial pattern (cmp [esi+14h], 10h) returns 30+ hits clustered in sub_5644AC90 / sub_5644AFB0 / sub_5644B110 / sub_5644B350 — but analyze_function on sub_5644AC90 reveals this is MSVC std::string SSO reallocation (v3+16=size, v3+20=capacity, 0x10=16-byte SSO threshold), not PSM 0x10 dispatch. The pattern is a MSVC std::string idiom and produces noise across any C++ binary. Pivoting to debug-string-based search per blockers.md Slice A-alt.

PSMSerializeIn.role: on-disk → in-memory deserializer; primary candidate for PSM type code 0x10 dispatch trace

PSMSerializeOut.role: in-memory → on-disk serializer; secondary dispatch trace candidate

summary: DEBUG STRINGS confirm the PSM serialization entry points. PSMSerializeIn at sub_564915E0 is the canonical reverse-engineering target for Slice B — it dispatches incoming PSM records by type code. Next session: analyze_function sub_564915E0 → find the type-code dispatch switch → identify the 0x10 case branch → trace to factory / sub-record handler.

Slice A complete with concrete candidates: PSMSerializeIn (sub_564915E0) + PSMSerializeOut (sub_56491E80) + 7 PSM stream-table readers. False-positive lesson: cmp [eXX+14h], 10h is MSVC std::string SSO check, NOT PSM dispatch — skip this pattern in future searches. Hand-off to Slice B: analyze PSMSerializeIn, find PSM type code switch, locate 0x10 branch.

Phase 20 Slice A landed concrete handoff targets. Next session entry point: select_instance(13346) + analyze_function("sub_564915E0"). Look for switch / jump table indexed on packed-byte type_code; the 0x10 branch should call (or fall through to) a sub-record factory. If PSMSerializeIn is the wrong layer (too generic), candidates 2/3 are sub_5646AE30 (has PSMSTATE_UNINITIALIZED assertion, suggests state-machine read) and sub_5647A630 (PSMsegmenttable reader, may know record-type taxonomy). Estimated 2-3 more sessions to AC1 (RAD class identity) + AC2 (sub-kind discriminator) + AC3 (sub-kind enumeration).

PSMSerializeIn.interpretation: PSM records have OID + nType pair; nType is the type code (0x10 for sub-record family); guidtab.h is the CLSID lookup table referenced when nType is unknown / invalid

GUIDTAB_rdata.layout: sequential array of (16-byte GUID + 4-byte size or class_id tail = 20 bytes per entry)
GUIDTAB_rdata.note: 47FCC337 absent; this is NOT a 1:1 CLSID-to-type-code mapping by Data1

GUIDTAB_data.note: Each tuple has 0x340 (832 decimal) tail — likely class instance size or version, not type code

summary: Slice B partial: PSMSerializeIn entry confirmed; GUIDTAB master CLSID array found at .rdata:0x566642E4 AND .data:0x5667B388. JStyleOverride (47FCC338) presence in the table independently confirms Phase 16 reverse engineering. NEXT: find xref to dword_566642E4 / 5667B388 to identify dispatch function, then trace nType=0x10 to its CLSID entry.

Independent IDA cross-check: JStyleOverride CLSID 47FCC338 is present in radsrvitem.dll GUIDTAB at .rdata:0x566642E4+offset and .data:0x5667B41C. This strengthens the Phase 16 reverse engineering chain (radsrvitem.dll -> J2DSrv.dll -> JUTIL.dll -> style.dll) and shows that PSM 0x0010 should be reachable via the same table lookup mechanism, even though by-position the table is curated (gaps at 47FCC337 / 47FCC33A).

Slice B 60% complete. Next session entry: find xrefs to dword_566642E4 (.rdata GUIDTAB) and 5667B388 (.data table) to identify the dispatch / lookup function. Once located, find how nType (the PSM type code) maps into the table to extract CLSID for nType=0x10. Estimated 1-2 more sessions to Slice C/D (Read/IO function + sub-kind discriminator).

guidtab_indirect_addressing.interpretation: The GUIDTAB is accessed indirectly via runtime computation (e.g. base + nType * 20) — no static literal address xref. Dispatch logic must be located by reading the function bodies that use the GUIDTAB.

guidtab_multiple_families.observation: .rdata:0x56664310 contains GUID 4BDD0136-72A0-11D0F70C-82EA2-2D65236 INTERLEAVED with the 47FCC33X siblings
guidtab_multiple_families.interpretation: The GUIDTAB is NOT a single contiguous 47FCC33X family — it interleaves multiple distinct CLSID prefixes. PSM 0x0010 RAD class belongs to ONE of these other prefixes, not 47FCC33X. This independently re-confirms Phase 19 RAD sibling probe null result.

jstyleoverride_relocation.corrected: .data:0x5667B428 (47FCC338 is the first dword of entry; previous bytes are tail of 47FCC336 entry)
jstyleoverride_relocation.entry_layout: Confirmed: entries are 20 bytes (16-byte GUID + 4-byte 0x340 tail); entries 47FCC330..47FCC336 are sequential, then 47FCC338 (skipping 337), then 47FCC339, then 47FCC33B (skipping 33A) — table has documented gaps

summary: Slice B Part 4 nailed two negative results: (1) GUIDTAB has no static xrefs, so dispatch is runtime-computed; (2) the table interleaves multiple CLSID families and 47FCC33X is just one family. PSM 0x0010's RAD class is in a different family entirely. Next session must (a) read a larger .rdata slice to enumerate all CLSID prefixes in the table, (b) find PSMSerializeIn's dispatch path (truncated at 397 bytes in initial analyze_function), (c) check callees sub_56490B00 / sub_56491050 / sub_5648A9D0 / sub_5647AFB0 for sub-record handler routing.

Phase 20 has now landed Slice A (100%) + Slice B (60-70%). Progress is FASTER than estimated (single Cursor session covered what was projected as 1-2 sessions). Remaining Slice B work: trace PSMSerializeIn dispatch path or its CLSID-resolving callee. Slice C-G unchanged.

Reverse engineering rule confirmed: skip 'cmp [eX+14h], 10h' (MSVC std::string SSO). Use debug strings + .rdata CLSID table reads instead.

GUIDTAB locations:
- .rdata:0x566642E4 (canonical, read-only)
- .data:0x5667B388 (initialized copy)

Hand-off: select_instance(13346) + read_struct(0x566642E4, ~500 bytes) to enumerate CLSID families; analyze callees of PSMSerializeIn for sub-record routing.

findings.guidtab_layout: NOT uniform 20-byte entries. py_eval-based read of 800 bytes from .rdata:0x566642E4 shows 47FCC331/332/333/334/336/339... entries at uneven byte offsets (gaps 40 / 40 / >40 between siblings), with many interleaved non-47FCC33X CLSIDs (00086B66-0136-4BDD, E3827C80-FC85-11D0, 11CF1B7E-D08E-0008, etc.). The table is a complex structured array, not a flat array indexed by nType.

sub_56491050.interpretation: Calls vtable[+20] on its argument class — likely returns a property (possibly nType?) from a stream/record class. Called from PSMSerializeIn at 0x564917DE and 0x56491BD2 (right before the OID/nType debug string)
sub_56491050.not_dispatch: This is the property reader, not the nType→CLSID dispatcher itself

summary: Slice B Part 5: GUIDTAB read-out via py_eval confirms it's NOT a uniform 20-byte array but a complex structured table interleaving many CLSID families. The 47FCC33X siblings are sparse within a larger collection. nType-to-CLSID dispatch logic must be in the truncated portion of PSMSerializeIn (initial analyze returned only 397/2206 bytes). Next session: increase decompile budget or read assembly directly to capture the actual dispatch switch / table lookup.

Phase 20 Session 2 closes with: Slice A 100% + Slice B ~70% complete. CONFIRMED facts: PSMSerializeIn (sub_564915E0, 2206 bytes), PSMSerializeOut (sub_56491E80), 7 PSM stream-table reader functions, GUIDTAB at .rdata:0x566642E4 + .data:0x5667B388 (multi-family complex layout), Phase 16 47FCC338 JStyleOverride independently cross-validated at .data:0x5667B428. CONFIRMED-NEGATIVE: cmp [eX+14h], 10h is MSVC std::string SSO (skip in future searches); 47FCC33X is just one of many CLSID families (Phase 19 RAD sibling probe null result re-validated). NEXT-SESSION-FIRST-ACTION: select_instance(13346) + py_eval to read PSMSerializeIn full disassembly (idc.GetDisasm) for the truncated dispatch portion 0x564917E0..0x56491BD0, OR use py_eval with idc.get_func_attr to slice the function into chunks. Look for jump table, switch on nType, or table indexing into 0x566642E4. Estimated 1-2 more sessions to AC1-AC4.

Resumed Phase 20 from session2 handoff after MCP user confirmation '按推荐方案继续'. Re-read plan/progress/verification and confirmed next action: select_instance(13346) + py_eval on PSMSerializeIn sub_564915E0 truncated dispatch range. list_instances confirmed the same 12 reachable IDA instances; no new instance loaded.

findings.psm_type_extract: PSMSerializeIn reads an existing packed type from [record+8] via 'shr eax, 6; and eax, 3FFFh' at 0x564918DE..0x564918EA and compares it with incoming nType in dx at 0x564918EF.
findings.type_lookup: When packed type != incoming nType, PSMSerializeIn calls sub_564689C0(dword_567DDC90, type, &entry) and walks entry->parent at +0x12 until it reaches a matching table entry; sub_564689C0 is PersistTypeTable indexed lookup: if index < *(this+0x12), return *(this+0x0C)[index].
findings.factory_bridge: sub_56490B00 is the object bridge called from PSMSerializeIn at 0x56491A3C; it delegates to sub_5647CE40(a1,0,1,&riid,out), which appears as a local stub returning E_NOTIMPL in radsrvitem.dll, so the concrete class remains in the target COM/RAD DLL.

summary: The dispatch path is no longer opaque: PSMSerializeIn uses a runtime-initialized PersistTypeTable<PersistComTypeEntry>, not a flat GUIDTAB address xref. The type code is a 14-bit index into that table; parent-chain validation explains why some records can legally deserialize under an ancestor/alias type.

tool: sub_56445C90 -> sub_56455D10 -> sub_5647C1E0 + static table dump

findings.static_table: sub_56445C90 initializes the PersistTypeTable by calling sub_56455D10(281, dword_5667B068, sub_56471660, 0). sub_56455D10 walks 281 static entries at 20-byte stride: 16-byte GUID + tail byte16 + tail byte17 + tail word18.
findings.entry_insertion: sub_5647C1E0 stores the sequential table index at entry+0x10, the parent index at entry+0x12, tail16 at entry+0x14, tail17 at entry+0x16, and copies the 16-byte GUID to entry+0x00.
findings.psm_0x0010: Static entry index 0x0010 at radsrvitem.dll .data:0x5667B1A8 = GUID 1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046, tail16=0x40, tail17=0x06, parent=0x0115.
findings.parent_alias: Static entry index 0x0115 at radsrvitem.dll .data:0x5667C60C uses the same GUID 1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046, tail16=0xC0, tail17=0x06, parent=0x0000. Therefore PSM 0x0010 is a child/alias entry for the same COM/RAD GUID, not an unrelated sibling class.

summary: Slice B reached the first concrete AC1 identity artifact: PSM type 0x0010 maps to GUID 1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046 via the radsrvitem PersistTypeTable. Remaining AC1 gap: recover the human class name / concrete factory in the target DLL.

tool: GUID byte-pattern search across existing IDA instances + registry probe

findings.registry: reg query HKCR\\CLSID\\{1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046} returned key not found; no local COM registration evidence.

summary: Best current target DLL evidence points to style.dll because the same GUID occurs in style.dll .rdata and was not found in the checked SmartPlant/J2D siblings. However style.dll has no static xrefs to 0x10068F44 and no registry ProgID/class-name hit yet, so class name remains unresolved.

tool: select_instance(13348) + DllGetClassObject analysis + GUID byte-pattern comparison

findings.style_exports: style.dll exports DllGetClassObject at 0x100047B0 plus style helpers including HCreateOverrideStyle, HGetOverrideStyle, HloadOverrideData, etc.
findings.dll_get_class_object: DllGetClassObject is a long direct CLSID comparison chain using sub_10001070(rclsid, &GUID) and allocating 0x10-byte class factories via sub_100013E0/sub_10001600/...; it compares against GUID globals in the 0x10066xxx/0x10067xxx ranges, not 0x10068F44.
findings.jstyleoverride_control: JStyleOverride GUID 47FCC338-2D0F-11D0-A1FF-080036A1CF02 is present at style.dll .rdata:0x10066B64 and is one of the DllGetClassObject class-map GUIDs; its factory path calls sub_10001600. This confirms the class-map method works for known Phase 16 CLSIDs.
findings.psm0010_negative: PSM 0x0010 GUID 1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046 is present only at style.dll .rdata:0x10068F44 in this instance, has no static xrefs, and no pointer-to-GUID matches. It is therefore not currently an activatable style.dll CLSID in DllGetClassObject; treat it as a persist/interface type GUID until another factory path is found.
findings.style_override_export_probe: HloadOverrideData at 0x10041A60 copies tagStyleOverride-like fields under bit flags from one struct to another and is called by HGetOverrideStyle overloads. It does not identify the PSM 0x0010 class/factory directly.

summary: The style.dll hit is real but negative for direct CLSID factory identity: unlike JStyleOverride, the PSM 0x0010 GUID is not in DllGetClassObject. Next recommended step: search for runtime registration or persist-type factory paths outside DllGetClassObject, especially functions that consume the 0x10068xxx GUID block or style.dll type-library metadata.

tool: repo metadata search + style.dll code-reference scan + HKCR Interface query

findings.repo_metadata: No style.dll/style.tlb/IDL metadata exists in the workspace; only Phase 16 docs reference style/JStyleOverride. rg found no prior 1D1928C0 mention outside the new progress entries.
findings.style_segments: style.dll IDA instance exposes .text/.idata/.rdata/.data only; no loaded resource segment/type-library resource visible from IDA segment list.
findings.code_refs_to_10068_block: Scanning style.dll executable segments for rendered operands containing 10068 found only references to unk_10068AD4 and unk_10068B84 (e.g. sub_1003E0D0, sub_10059060, sub_10059D00). No code reference to 0x10068F44 was found.
findings.registry_interface: reg query HKCR\\Interface\\{1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046} returned key not found.

summary: Additional runtime/type-library angle also produced negative evidence: 0x10068F44 appears to be an unreferenced GUID constant in the loaded style.dll IDB, not a directly used QueryInterface IID or class factory GUID. This weakens the 'style.dll contains the concrete 0x0010 class' hypothesis; the next viable path is to trace radsrvitem's factory bridge/sub_5647CE40 import mechanics or recover the persist type name from external metadata not currently loaded.

tool: analyze_function callers of sub_5647CE40/sub_5647CA50 + import scan + callback xrefs

findings.sub_5647CE40: Direct callers (sub_56490B00, sub_56512F70, sub_5655D0D0/sub_5655D120/sub_5655D170) use it as a generic GUID/interface object creation helper, but the function body itself is a 19-byte E_NOTIMPL-style stub (*out=0; return 0x80004001).
findings.co_create_instance: radsrvitem.dll imports ole32!CoCreateInstance at IAT 0x5665E568, but the direct CoCreateInstance wrappers sub_56442AC0/sub_56442B70 use CLSID 5D22F258-8B6F-44E0-A639-D3C5F47BBA29 and are not the PSM 0x0010 persist-table path.
findings.persist_callback: sub_56445C90 initializes dword_567DDC60 to sub_56471660 via sub_56455D10(281, dword_5667B068, sub_56471660, 0). xrefs show dword_567DDC60 is invoked in sub_56469100 after sub_56468B30 resolves a record/type entry.
findings.sub_56471660: This callback switches on tail17/category values 1-6,8-12 and calls sub_5647CA50(a1,a2,out,category). PSM 0x0010 has tail17=0x06, so it is in the accepted category set.
findings.sub_5647CA50: sub_5647CA50 is also an E_NOTIMPL default stub, but it appears as a virtual/table slot in IGDSFactory* vtables (e.g. around 0x566667B0) and is called as a fallback from many factory-like paths; real creation can instead flow through host object virtual slots, especially sub_56468B30's v8-vtable QueryInterface/call path and sub_56469100's dword_567DDC60 callback path.
findings.sub_56468B30: This function resolves an existing record/type via sub_56479970 + sub_56468930, uses packed type bits and table entry flags, and can call the resolved object's vtable directly when entry flags permit. It only falls back to sub_5647CA50 for unsupported paths.

summary: Factory bridge is partially understood: the visible sub_5647CE40/sub_5647CA50 bodies are default unsupported stubs, not the concrete 0x0010 class factory. The real creation route is likely runtime host/vtable mediated through sub_56469100/sub_56468B30 and the dword_567DDC60 callback, with tail17=0x06 selecting an accepted factory category. Remaining gap: identify which vtable/object supplies the concrete creation for GUID 1D1928C0 / type 0x0010, or prove this family is intentionally raw/audit-only because the concrete factory is unavailable in the loaded IDBs.

findings.sub_56479970: This is an OID/slot resolver, not a class factory: it splits a packed object id into page = a2 >> 13 and slot = a2 & 0x1FFF, ensures the page is loaded via sub_5647A7B0, then returns a record slot pointer via sub_564798D0(slot).
findings.sub_56468B30_v8: In sub_56468B30, v8 = *record_slot from sub_56479970. The vtable call at 0x56468D38/0x56468D3E is therefore an operation on an already resolved object/record, guarded by type-entry flags, not direct creation of the GUID 1D1928C0 class.
findings.lazy_cluster_path: If the object/type is not directly usable, sub_56468B30 calls sub_5648BE20/sub_5648C170 against a registry/cluster table indexed by the upper packed type bits. sub_5648BE20 can populate missing entries via sub_5648BBA0.
findings.sub_5648BBA0: This constructs/uses a SerialCluster via sub_56493840, obtains backing objects through sub_56469790, then calls sub_56490B30/sub_56491090 to access named persisted children through vtable slots 12/16/64/68. This is a storage/cluster lazy-load path, not a simple CLSID factory.
findings.serialcluster: sub_56493840 initializes SerialCluster::vftable and stores category/cluster metadata; it does not expose a human class name for PSM 0x0010.

summary: The vtable source is now mostly characterized: sub_56468B30 works from packed OID -> record slot -> existing object/type entry, with SerialCluster lazy-load fallback. This explains why direct CLSID/factory searches have not found a concrete class. Remaining AC1 gap narrows to naming the persisted type behind GUID 1D1928C0, not finding a normal COM class factory.

Created partial Phase 20 analysis doc. It records confirmed GUID/type-table identity for PSM 0x0010 (GUID 1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046, parent alias 0x0115, tail17=0x06), PSMSerializeIn dispatch evidence, factory/vtable bridge findings, style.dll negative evidence, cross-fixture relationship, address index, known unknowns, and Phase 21 implications. Status is explicitly partial: human persisted type name, concrete Read/DoIO function, and sub-kind discriminator remain unresolved.

deferred: human persisted type name / concrete RAD class name
phase14_baselines_preserved: not rerun in this docs-only partial closeout
phase15_audit_preserved: not rerun in this docs-only partial closeout
phase16_jstyle_preserved: not rerun in this docs-only partial closeout
phase17_primitive_arc_removed: not rerun in this docs-only partial closeout
phase18_audit_preserved: not rerun in this docs-only partial closeout
phase19_leading_word_preserved: not rerun in this docs-only partial closeout
summary: Partial AC accepted after repeated user continue confirmations. Phase 20 closes the current reverse-engineering loop with confirmed persisted GUID/type-table identity for PSM 0x0010, but defers human class name, Read/DoIO, and sub-kind discriminator. Do not implement typed DTO from this evidence alone.

findings.local_types: No IDA local type entries matched 1D1928, Persist, Style, or Override in style.dll.
findings.rendered_text: No style.dll rendered-listing hits for 1D1928 or 1D1928C0.
findings.guid_neighborhood: style.dll .rdata:0x10068F44 contains GUID 1D1928C0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046, immediately followed by 09D6BBB0-0000-0000-C000-000000000046 and 8EC51800-0000-0000-C000-000000000046; all three have zero direct xrefs for the target and no names.
findings.adjacent_guid_arrays: Nearby referenced GUID-array addresses 0x10068AD4 and 0x10068B84 are used by default style / exception-style creation functions (HGetDimExceptionStyle, sub_1003E0D0, sub_10059060, sub_10059D00), but they do not reference 1D1928C0.
findings.registry: HKCR CLSID/Interface/TypeLib lookups for 1D1928C0, 09D6BBB0, and 8EC51800 returned key-not-found.
findings.repo_metadata: No .tlb/.idl/.drx/.pdb/.lib/.exp/.map/.def metadata files found in the repo/dlls workspace.

summary: Follow-up of the recommended metadata/RTTI/local-type path did not recover a human persisted type name. Current conclusion remains: 1D1928C0 is a persisted/interface GUID constant present in style.dll but not an activatable/named class in the loaded IDB; typed DTO remains blocked on stronger Read/DoIO evidence.
